factors.name: Risk-on sentiment from Iran deal
factors.summary: US-Iran peace deal ~95% complete with Strait of Hormuz reopening hopes driving broad equity relief. ES +0.4%, NQ +0.6% in Globex, with weaker USD and falling commodity prices supporting tech multiples.

factors.name: Oil crash (-5%) & energy deflation
factors.summary: Brent crude below $100/bbl, WTI surrendering $90 threshold on Hormuz deal expectations. Lower energy inflation supports Fed-hold/cut narrative, bullish for long-duration growth stocks.

factors.name: USD weakness
factors.summary: Dollar sliding on risk-on mood and peace deal optimism (EUR/USD near 1.1640, AUD/USD above 0.7150). Weaker USD historically boosts NQ due to international revenue exposure of mega-caps.

factors.name: Real yields direction unclear
factors.summary: ECB Lagarde flagged June inflation forecast revision upward, and some policymakers signaling June hike unavoidable if Hormuz stays shut. Conflicting signals: oil falling (deflationary) vs ECB hawkishness (higher rates). 10Y move not detailed in news.

factors.name: No major US macro data this week
factors.summary: Today (May 25) is Memorial Day with US markets closed for much of trading. No CPI, PCE, NFP, or FOMC this week—removes near-term rate-shock risk but keeps chop potential.

factors.name: Price action already +0.6% on Globex
factors.summary: NQ rallied 60bps overnight on Iran deal news. Current 4h change only -0.58%, suggesting intraday volatility/chop rather than sustained momentum continuation.

factors.name: Mega-cap earnings cycle complete
factors.summary: No mention of upcoming AAPL, MSFT, GOOGL, AMZN, NVDA earnings in this news window. Summer earnings drought reduces single-name catalyst risk but also removes upside driver.

factors.name: Crypto correlation positive
factors.summary: Bitcoin bounces on Trump peace deal announcements and weaker USD. BTC-NQ correlation typically positive on risk-on days; crypto tailwind supports sentiment.

factors.name: Deal uncertainty & Trump opacity
factors.summary: Trump stated deal 'isn't even fully negotiated yet' and blockade stays until formal agreement signed. Nuclear/sanctions gaps remain; signing days away. Risk of surprise disappointment.

factors.name: Thin Asian liquidity
factors.summary: Hong Kong markets closed May 25; sparse calendar and holiday-thin tape increase chop risk and reduce conviction on directional moves.

screening.narrative: NAS100 opened the week with +0.6% Globex rally driven by US-Iran peace deal optimism (Strait of Hormuz reopening hopes), falling oil (-5%), and weaker USD—classic risk-on unwind. However, current price action shows only -0.58% 4h change despite overnight strength, suggesting intraday chop and consolidation rather than a sustained push higher. ECB inflation guidance and cautious Trump messaging on deal completion inject uncertainty. Move is already partially in, reducing first-cycle conviction.
catalysts_ahead: Formal US-Iran deal announcement (days to weeks; near-term binary)
ECB June 11 rate decision & Lagarde inflation forecast (late May, could refresh rates narrative)
Next US CPI/PCE (early June; dormant this week due to Memorial Day)
FOMC next meeting (timing TBD; major risk for long-duration equities if hawkish surprise)
Mega-cap earnings restart (AAPL, MSFT, GOOGL, AMZN, NVDA in coming weeks)
screening.risk_warning: If Iran deal stalls or Trump backtracks, oil rebounds sharply (+$5-10/bbl possible), pushing real yields higher and compressing NQ multiples. ECB June hike signal could also re-steepen curves and hurt long-duration tech. Thin Asian holiday liquidity increases whipsaw risk; wait for US cash open confirmation of direction. Move already +0.6% overnight—late entry chasers face pullback risk.
screening.entry_guidance: Move is in PHASE_2 (intraday chop post-gap-up). Avoid chasing above 29,700. Consider dip-buy on retest of 29,200-29,350 if Hormuz deal chatter remains positive and 10Y does not spike. Wait for US cash open (late Monday) to confirm momentum before sizing.
